title: BenQ’s 4100i Projector Brings Cinematic Picture Quality to Your Living Room
gist: Wired's 8/10 review of the $2,999 BenQ W4100i positions it just below the premium home-theater projector tier, with AI assistant integration (Google Gemini vs Google Assistant) emerging as a key differentiator and the segment crowded by comparable Leica, Epson, and Samsung offerings near the $3,000 price point.

insights.claim: The ~$3,000 home-theater projector tier is densely contested, with the BenQ W4100i ($2,999), the similarly priced Leica Cine Play 1, the $3,800 Epson Lifestudio Grand Plus, and Epson Pro Cinema LS9000 all competing, and BenQ rated just below the category leaders.
insights.speaker: John Brandon
insights.so_what: A crowded premium projector field signals pricing competition and the difficulty of differentiation in high-end home theater hardware.
insights.locator: The W4100i has many excellent rivals

insights.claim: The Epson Lifestudio Grand Plus's newer Google TV platform includes built-in Gemini support that the BenQ W4100i's older Android TV system lacks, with the reviewer noting Google Assistant is less advanced than Gemini.
insights.speaker: John Brandon
insights.so_what: AI assistant capability is becoming a competitive axis even in projectors; Google's Gemini integration on smart-TV OS could pull premium device sales.
insights.locator: The OS setup was the easiest

insights.claim: The BenQ W4100i's easiest setup path works only via Google login on Android phones and does not work on iPhone, requiring Apple users to use a manual workaround.
insights.speaker: John Brandon
insights.so_what: Smart home device onboarding increasingly favors the user's existing platform; cross-ecosystem friction shapes purchase satisfaction.
insights.quote: This easy setup does not work on an iPhone, so you have to initiate setup using your Google login.

themes.theme: Projectors targeting cord-cutting streamers and gamers

insights.claim: The projector is explicitly pitched at the Netflix and streaming crowd plus gamers, supporting high-frame-rate 240 Hz gaming and 24P cinematic playback, framing home projectors as a replacement for the theater and TV.
insights.speaker: John Brandon
insights.so_what: Home-theater projectors riding streaming and gaming demand point to a content-driven device category, complementary to OTT subscription growth.
insights.quote: For the Netflix crowd, setting up a movie theater in your house makes perfect sense.

themes.theme: Spec benchmarks for premium home theater

insights.claim: The BenQ W4100i delivers 3,200 lumens, 100 percent coverage of both Rec.709 and DCI-P3 color gamuts, and HDR10+ support, yet still loses on color vibrancy to Samsung's The Frame Pro 2026 and the Epson and Leica rivals.
insights.speaker: John Brandon
insights.so_what: Full color-gamut specs no longer guarantee category leadership; real-world image processing differentiates premium displays.
insights.locator: Specs on the BenQ W4100i are impressive
